_meta.notes: Fictional but realistic manufacturing plant. Equipment uses ISA-95 tag format. Plant: Northgate Automotive, Line 4 (body panels), Line 7 (paint shop).

test_cases.description: Basic SOP lookup — operator needs a specific procedure step

input.question: What is the correct torque setting for the wheel hub bolts on AT-301?

test_cases.mock_context: SOP-WH-401 Rev 2.1: Wheel hub assembly. Step 4: Torque wheel hub bolts to 185 Nm in a star pattern. Re-check torque after first 50 cycles. Do not use impact wrench for final torque.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Answer states 185 Nm and mentions star pattern. Cites SOP-WH-401.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Wrong torque value, no torque value given, or answer is vague prose with no actionable instruction.

test_cases.description: Safety question — operator asks about a chemical hazard

input.question: What PPE is required when handling the primer coat on Line 7?

test_cases.mock_context: MSDS-Primer-L7 Rev 1.0: Handling requirements. Mandatory PPE: nitrile gloves (min 0.2mm thickness), full face shield, chemical-resistant apron, forced-air respirator. Do NOT use dust masks — vapour protection required.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Lists at least 3 correct PPE items. Mentions respirator specifically (not dust mask).
test_cases.fail_criteria: Mentions dust mask as acceptable. Omits respirator. Lists fewer than 2 PPE items.

test_cases.description: Revision-aware retrieval — operator should get latest not archived version

input.question: What is the weld dwell time for the B-pillar on Line 4?

test_cases.mock_context: WI-WR-401 Rev 3.0 (CURRENT): B-pillar weld parameters. Dwell time: 420ms. Current: 9.2 kA. [ARCHIVED: WI-WR-401 Rev 2.0: Dwell time: 380ms. Current: 8.8 kA — superseded after quality review Apr 2024]
test_cases.pass_criteria: Returns 420ms from Rev 3.0. Does not mention the old 380ms value.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns 380ms (stale data). Returns both values without indicating which is current. No revision cited.

test_cases.description: No-data handling — question about equipment with no docs uploaded

input.question: What is the maintenance interval for the conveyor motor on Line 2?

expected.tone: clear, does not invent an answer

test_cases.pass_criteria: Clearly states no documents found for CM-201. Does NOT invent a plausible maintenance interval.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Invents a maintenance interval (e.g. 'typically every 3 months'). Gives a generic answer not grounded in documents.

test_cases.description: Multi-step procedure — answer should be structured as steps

input.question: How do I reset the weld controller on WR-401 after a fault?

test_cases.mock_context: SOP-WR-401 Rev 1.2: Fault recovery. Step 1: Press and hold E-STOP for 3 seconds. Step 2: Open controller panel (key switch to MAINT). Step 3: Navigate to FAULT LOG, clear active faults. Step 4: Close panel, return key to RUN. Step 5: Press RESET on HMI. Step 6: Run one test cycle before resuming production.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Returns numbered steps. E-STOP is step 1 or early. Test cycle mentioned at end. Order is logical.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Steps out of order. Key steps (E-STOP, test cycle) omitted. Answer is unstructured prose.

test_cases.description: Numerical specification — answer must be precise not approximate

input.question: What is the spatter index threshold that triggers a quality alarm on WR-401?

test_cases.mock_context: NCR-WR-401-2024-03: Quality control parameters. Spatter index alarm threshold: 3.5. Auto-quarantine triggered at: 5.0. Last calibration: March 2024.
test_cases.pass_criteria: States 3.5 as the alarm threshold. Does not round or approximate.
test_cases.fail_criteria: States wrong number. Uses vague language like 'around 3 to 4'. Does not distinguish alarm threshold from quarantine threshold.

test_cases.description: Cross-document reasoning — answer requires combining two sources

input.question: Can I use the standard weld wire for the roof panel or does it need the high-tensile grade?

test_cases.mock_context: WI-WR-401 Rev 3.0: Roof panel welding requires ER70S-6 high-tensile wire (min yield 490 MPa). Do NOT substitute standard ER70S-2. [Material Spec MS-401 Rev 1.1: Standard wire ER70S-2 is approved for body panels only, NOT structural panels. Roof panels are classified structural.]
test_cases.pass_criteria: Clearly states high-tensile ER70S-6 is required. Explains roof panels are structural. Does not leave ambiguity.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Says either wire is acceptable. Omits the structural classification. Gives vague answer.

test_cases.description: Alarm frequency question — how many times did something happen on a shift

input.question: How many times did WR-401 alarm last night shift?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Night 2024-04-20: 22:15 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator. 23:40 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator. 02:10 WR-401 weld quality alarm — line paused 8 mins. 04:55 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator.
test_cases.pass_criteria: States 4 alarms (or 3 spatter + 1 quality). Lists times or types. Does not confuse with other equipment.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Wrong count. Includes alarms from other equipment. No breakdown of alarm types.

test_cases.description: Shift handover summary — what happened across a full shift

input.question: Summarise what happened on Line 4 during the afternoon shift

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Afternoon 2024-04-20 Line 4: 14:30 WR-401 spatter alarm, cleared. 16:15 Conveyor speed reduced to 85% for panel alignment issue. 17:00 Panel alignment corrected, speed restored. 19:45 WR-401 weld quality alarm, quarantined 6 panels. 21:30 End of shift — 847 panels completed, 6 quarantined.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Covers: WR-401 alarms, conveyor issue, panel quarantine count, production count. Chronological or grouped by type.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Omits quarantine event. Wrong panel count. Mixes in events from other shifts.

test_cases.description: Operator action tracking — what did a specific operator do

input.question: What actions did the night shift operator take on WR-401?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Night 2024-04-20: 22:15 WR-401 spatter alarm — Operator J.Malik cleared alarm, cleaned spatter shield. 01:20 WR-401 preventive check — J.Malik checked wire feed tension, adjusted to spec. 03:45 WR-401 weld quality low — J.Malik adjusted tip-to-work distance, 2 test welds, resumed.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Names J.Malik. Lists at least 3 distinct actions with approximate times. Does not invent actions.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Wrong operator name. Omits tip-to-work adjustment. Invents actions not in the log.

test_cases.description: Time-range filtering — must only return events within the window

input.question: Did anything happen with PC-701 between 2am and 4am?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Night 2024-04-20: 00:30 PC-701 paint viscosity high — adjusted. 02:45 PC-701 nozzle clog alarm — nozzle cleaned, 4 mins downtime. 03:55 PC-701 temperature probe reading high — fan speed increased. 05:10 PC-701 viscosity recheck — within spec.

expected.format: events within the time window only

test_cases.pass_criteria: Returns only the 02:45 and 03:55 events. Does NOT include the 00:30 or 05:10 events.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Includes events outside the 02:00–04:00 window. Returns all events and ignores time filter.

test_cases.description: No events in window — honest answer when nothing happened

input.question: Were there any alarms on CM-301 during the day shift?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Day 2024-04-20 Line 4: No alarms recorded for CM-301. Equipment status: normal throughout shift.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Clearly states no alarms for CM-301 on that shift. Confident, not hedging.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Hedges with 'I couldn't determine'. Returns events from other equipment. Invents an alarm.

test_cases.description: Pattern detection across multiple days — should identify a trend

input.question: Has WR-401 been alarming more than usual this week?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift logs Apr 14-20: Mon: 1 alarm. Tue: 1 alarm. Wed: 3 alarms. Thu: 4 alarms. Fri: 4 alarms. Sat: 5 alarms. Avg prior 4 weeks: 1.2 alarms/day.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Identifies the increasing trend. Compares to prior average. Flags concern. Does not dismiss as normal.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Says activity is normal. Does not identify the Wed-onwards escalation. No comparison to baseline.

test_cases.description: Production count question — quantitative extraction from shift log

input.question: How many panels were produced on Line 4 today and how many were quarantined?

test_cases.mock_context: Day shift 2024-04-20 Line 4: Total panels completed: 1,284. Quarantined (weld quality): 11 panels. Scrap: 2 panels. First-pass yield: 99.0%. Night shift: 847 panels, 6 quarantined.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Returns 1,284 produced and 11 quarantined for day shift. Does not mix in night shift numbers (847).
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns night shift numbers instead. Adds day + night totals together without labelling. Omits quarantine count.

test_cases.description: Classic repeat failure — agent should identify pattern and escalation

input.incident: WR-401 welding robot on Line 4 just triggered a weld quality alarm. Spatter index reading 4.8. This is the third time this week.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 15 02:00 WR-401 spatter alarm, operator cleaned shield. Apr 17 14:30 WR-401 spatter alarm, operator cleaned shield. Apr 19 09:00 WR-401 weld quality alarm, spatter 4.8, 3 panels quarantined.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-WR-401: Last full service Mar 28. Wire liner replaced. Next service due May 28. Note: wire liner avg lifespan 6 weeks.
mock_context.sop: SOP-WR-401: Spatter index >3.5 = alarm. >5.0 = auto-quarantine. Recurring spatter within 7 days = inspect wire liner and tip.
mock_context.ncr: NCR-2024-08: Feb spatter recurrence — root cause was blocked wire liner causing inconsistent feed. Corrective action: monthly liner inspection.

expected.format: structured report with SOURCE DATA, ISSUE, IMPACT, CRITICALITY, ACTION sections

test_cases.pass_criteria: Identifies wire liner as likely root cause. References the Feb NCR as precedent. Recommends liner inspection. Assigns HIGH or CRITICAL criticality. Has all 5 report sections.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Does not mention wire liner. Treats each alarm as isolated event. Missing report sections. Invents data not in sources.

test_cases.description: No history — agent should state insufficient data, not hallucinate

input.incident: CM-301 conveyor motor on Line 3 just stopped. No movement, no alarm lights, just stopped.

expected.format: honest no-data response with recommended actions

test_cases.pass_criteria: Explicitly states no documents found for CM-301. Does NOT speculate about root cause. Recommends uploading maintenance records. Suggests immediate manual inspection.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Invents plausible-sounding root causes (overheating, bearing failure etc.) without document evidence. Uses hedged speculation ('probably', 'likely').

test_cases.description: Safety-critical incident — agent must assign correct criticality

input.incident: PC-701 paint booth on Line 7. Exhaust fan stopped working. Operators can smell solvent fumes. Line is still running.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 08:15 PC-701 exhaust fan fault — fan motor alarm. Operator acknowledged.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-PC-701: Exhaust fan last serviced Jan 2024. Fan belt condition: monitor.
mock_context.sop: SOP-PC-701: Exhaust fan failure = IMMEDIATE LINE STOP. Solvent exposure risk. Evacuate booth area. Do not restart until ventilation confirmed working. Notify safety officer.

test_cases.pass_criteria: Assigns CRITICAL. Immediate action includes line stop and evacuation. References SOP requirement. Notifies safety officer. Does NOT downgrade to monitor-and-continue.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Assigns anything below CRITICAL. Suggests continuing to run the line. Does not mention evacuation. Treats as routine maintenance item.

test_cases.description: Recent maintenance relevance — agent should connect recent work to current fault

input.incident: WR-401 welding robot. Wire feed stuttering and inconsistent arc since this morning. No spatter alarm yet but weld quality is degrading.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 06:30 WR-401 inconsistent arc, operator noted wire feed hesitation. Apr 20 09:00 Same issue persisting.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-WR-401 Apr 19: Preventive maintenance. Wire liner replaced. Wire spool changed. Note: new liner requires 2hr burn-in — first 50m may feed inconsistently.
mock_context.sop: SOP-WR-401: After wire liner replacement, run 50m burn-in before production. Wire feed inconsistency in first 2hrs after liner change is expected.

expected.format: structured report, connects maintenance to symptom

test_cases.pass_criteria: Connects yesterday's liner replacement to today's symptom. Cites the burn-in requirement from SOP. Assigns LOW or MEDIUM (not CRITICAL) because this is expected behaviour. Recommends completing the burn-in.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Does not connect maintenance to symptom. Assigns CRITICAL or HIGH. Recommends unnecessary repair work. Misses the burn-in procedure.

test_cases.description: Report structure completeness — all 5 sections must be present

input.incident: P-201 coolant pump tripped on thermal overload. Third trip this week.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 14, 16, 19: P-201 thermal overload trips. Each resolved by operator resetting breaker and cleaning cooling fan.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-P-201: Last service Feb 2024. Bearing temp normal. Cooling fan cleaned in Feb service.
mock_context.sop: SOP-P-201: Thermal overload = check cooling fan and motor windings. 3+ trips in 7 days = escalate to maintenance.
mock_context.ncr: NCR-2024-01: Jan thermal overload recurrence — root cause blocked cooling fan. Fan replaced.

test_cases.pass_criteria: All 5 report sections present. Each section has content. SOURCE DATA lists specific documents. CRITICALITY is HIGH or CRITICAL given 3 trips in 7 days.
test_cases.fail_criteria: One or more sections missing or empty. Report is free-form prose without section headers. SOURCE DATA section is empty.

test_cases.description: Conflicting signals — agent should reason about contradictions in data

input.incident: WR-401 spatter index spiked to 4.2 but visual weld inspection shows normal quality. Maintenance says they just cleaned everything. Confusing situation.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 11:00 WR-401 spatter alarm 4.2. Operator visual check: welds look normal. Apr 20 10:30 Maintenance completed full clean — tips, liner, shield.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-WR-401 Apr 20: Full tip and liner clean. All readings normal post-clean.
mock_context.sop: SOP-WR-401: Spatter index sensor located near shield. After shield clean, residue on sensor can cause false high readings for up to 30 mins.

test_cases.pass_criteria: Identifies sensor residue as likely cause of false reading. References SOP note about 30-min false high after clean. Assigns LOW criticality. Recommends waiting and re-checking, not escalating.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Ignores the conflicting signals. Treats the spike as a real fault. Misses the SOP note about sensor readings after cleaning. Assigns HIGH/CRITICAL incorrectly.
